# Test Review: journal/ui/widgets/create/

**Scope:** `lib/features/journal/ui/widgets/create/` and `test/features/journal/ui/widgets/create/`
**Generated by automated test-quality review (static analysis)**

---

## File inventory

| File | Lines | Has test? | Top issue |
|---|---|---|---|
| `lib/.../create_entry_action_button.dart` | 31 | Yes (`create_entry_action_button_test.dart`, 32 lines) | Only one test; missing onPressed/tap behavior test |
| `lib/.../create_entry_action_modal.dart` | 95 | Yes (`create_entry_action_modal_test.dart`, 288 lines) | 12× `pumpAndSettle` (×2 per test), ad-hoc `ProviderScope`/`MaterialApp` wrapping |
| `lib/.../create_entry_items.dart` | 395 | Yes (two test files – `create_entry_items_test.dart` 1133 + `modern_create_entry_items_test.dart` 1966) | **Two test files for one source file** violates one-test-file-per-source rule; `modern_create_entry_items_test.dart` is critically oversized (1966 lines) |
| `lib/.../create_menu_list_item.dart` | 77 | Yes (`create_menu_list_item_test.dart`, 329 lines) | 16× `pumpAndSettle` for simple stateless widget; no animations |

---

## File size / split opportunities

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/features/journal/ui/widgets/create/modern_create_entry_items_test.dart` is **1966 lines** — the single biggest test file flagged in scope. The file duplicates entire test infrastructure (6 independent `setUpAll`/`tearDownAll` blocks, each creating `JournalDb` + `EditorDb` in memory) for groups that could share a single shared fixture. Additionally, much of its content duplicates `create_entry_items_test.dart` for the same source file. **Split seams:** (a) Merge into `create_entry_items_test.dart` (one test file per source file), (b) collapse the 6 identical DB-setup blocks into a single file-level `setUpAll`.

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/features/journal/ui/widgets/create/create_entry_items_test.dart` is **1133 lines** and already covers `create_entry_items.dart`. The existence of `modern_create_entry_items_test.dart` as a **second test file for the same source file** (`create_entry_items.dart`) violates the one-test-file-per-source rule. The two files should be merged; tests that overlap should be deduplicated.

- [ ] **[LOW]** All four implementation files are well within 500-line guidance. No impl splits needed.

---

## Test quality improvements

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `modern_create_entry_items_test.dart:1573` — `await tester.pump(const Duration(seconds: 4))` is a **4-second real wait** buried inside `CreateTimerItem Widget Integration Tests / onTap executes and creates timer`. The comment explains it is waiting for the polling loop (`_waitForTimerAndScroll`) to time out. This is a fake-time violation: the test should mock `LinkedEntriesController` so the poll either immediately succeeds or be replaced by a unit test that exercises `_waitForTimerAndScroll` directly with a `FakeLinkedEntriesController` (which already exists in `create_entry_items_test.dart`).

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `modern_create_entry_items_test.dart` lines ~566–1003: The groups `ModernCreateAudioItem Tests`, `ModernCreateTextItem Tests`, `ModernImportImageItem Tests`, `ModernCreateScreenshotItem Tests` each contain three near-identical tests: `renders correctly`, `onTap callback exists` (which only asserts `item.onTap, isNotNull` and `item.icon == X`), and `passes linkedId and categoryId correctly` (which only asserts widget property access). The `onTap callback exists` and `passes ...correctly` variants are **constructor smoke / property-access tests with zero behavioral value** — they test that a field echoes back the constructor argument. These should be deleted or replaced with meaningful interaction tests.

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `modern_create_entry_items_test.dart`: 6 independent `setUpAll` blocks (lines 1478, 1644, 1721, 1810, 1864, 1918) each register identical `UpdateNotifications` + `JournalDb` + `EditorDb` + `EditorStateService` into `getIt`. This is severe copy-paste setup bloat. A shared file-level `setUpAll`/`tearDownAll` or a `_TestBench.create()` helper would eliminate ~120 lines of redundant boilerplate.

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `modern_create_entry_items_test.dart`: Inline `getIt.registerSingleton<JournalDb>(mockDb)` scattered across multiple individual test groups (lines 534, 1031, 1064, 1095, 1122, 1165, 1229, 1263) bypasses `setUpTestGetIt()` / `tearDownTestGetIt()` — violating the centralized GetIt rule. Some groups rely on `tearDown(() async { await getIt.reset(); })` which runs after each test but does not protect against cross-group contamination.

- [ ] **[MED]** `create_entry_action_modal_test.dart`: Every test body duplicates the same `ProviderScope` + `MaterialApp` + localization + `Builder` + `ElevatedButton` wrapper (lines 54–87, 91–123, 126–160, etc.). A shared `_pumpModal(tester, {linkedFromId, categoryId})` helper would reduce each test body to 3–5 lines and would comply with the "extract pump/setup helpers" guideline. Ad-hoc `MaterialApp` construction also violates the `makeTestableWidget()` rule.

- [ ] **[MED]** `create_entry_action_modal_test.dart:87` — `expect(find.byType(CreateMenuListItem), findsWidgets)` is a **near-smoke assertion**: it only checks "at least one item found" without verifying count, labels, or order. The test title is "shows modal with all menu items when events enabled" — the assertion does not verify "all".

- [ ] **[MED]** `create_menu_list_item_test.dart:255–279`: `'Row contains exactly 4 children'` — this tests widget-tree structure, not behavior. If the implementation is refactored to wrap the icon in another widget, the test breaks without any user-visible regression. Replace with a behavioral assertion (e.g., that icon, title, and trailing plus are all visible at specific positions).

- [ ] **[LOW]** `create_entry_action_button_test.dart`: Only one test — it verifies the design-system FAB type and size but never verifies that tapping the button calls `CreateEntryModal.show`. A second test covering the `onPressed` callback (by checking that a modal title appears after tap) would complete behavioral coverage.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `modern_create_entry_items_test.dart:736–740`: The test `'publishJournalFocus is not called when linked entry is null'` does nothing except create and immediately dispose a `ProviderContainer`. This is a zero-assertion test with a misleadingly informative title — it should be deleted.

---

## Generative (Glados) testing opportunities

No genuine candidates in this subdir. All public logic is widget `build` methods and navigation callbacks. The `_waitForTimerAndScroll` polling logic is impure (uses `Future.delayed` and `ProviderContainer.read`). No stateless pure transformations identified.

---

## Coverage / missing-behavior gaps

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `PasteImageItem` — the **paste-when-visible path** (clipboard contains image, user taps the item, `ref.read(provider.notifier).paste()` is called, sheet closes) is tested in `create_entry_items_test.dart` but the corresponding group in `modern_create_entry_items_test.dart` (lines 1914–1965) only tests the **hidden state** (`canPasteImage == false`). The visible + paste interaction test already exists in the other file but is behind a `// PasteImageItem tests skipped` comment in `modern_create_entry_items_test.dart` (line 1288). Consolidation into one file would make this clear.

- [ ] **[MED]** `CreateChecklistItem.onTap` — pop behavior is tested (`create_entry_items_test.dart:239–307`) but the test uses raw `showModalBottomSheet` wiring rather than `makeTestableWidgetWithScaffold`, leading to ad-hoc setup code.

- [ ] **[MED]** `FloatingAddActionButton` (`create_entry_action_button_test.dart`) — the `onPressed` path (which triggers `CreateEntryModal.show`) is not tested. Only widget type and size are checked.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `CreateEventItem` with `enableEventsFlag` transitioning from `true → false` (flag toggled off after being on). Only `false → true` transition is tested (line 1115–1156 of `modern_create_entry_items_test.dart`).

---

## Test execution speed opportunities

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `modern_create_entry_items_test.dart:1573`: `await tester.pump(const Duration(seconds: 4))` — **adds ~4 seconds** to every test run. Replace with a controlled linked-entries mock so the poll exits immediately (a `_FakeLinkedEntriesController` already exists in `create_entry_items_test.dart` for exactly this purpose).

- [ ] **[HIGH]** 6 isolated `setUpAll`/`tearDownAll` blocks in `modern_create_entry_items_test.dart` each open and close real in-memory SQLite databases. Each open/close cycle has measurable overhead. Merging into one shared setup would reduce SQLite open/close events from 6 pairs to 1 pair. **Estimated saving: 300–500 ms per full suite run** for this file alone.

- [ ] **[MED]** `create_entry_action_modal_test.dart`: 12 `pumpAndSettle` calls across 6 tests (2 per test — one for initial render, one after `tap('Open Modal')`). The initial `pumpAndSettle` before tap is unnecessary; `await tester.pump()` suffices for static widgets with no running animations. Each unnecessary `pumpAndSettle` adds up to 10 s of potential timeout risk.

- [ ] **[MED]** `create_menu_list_item_test.dart`: 16 `pumpAndSettle` calls for a fully stateless widget with no animations. Every call should be `await tester.pump()` — `pumpAndSettle` provides zero benefit here and risks hanging on framework animation if test environment changes.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `modern_create_entry_items_test.dart:1149`, `1206`: two `pumpAndSettle` calls inside flag-toggle tests that involve `StreamController` events. These are legitimate (waiting for stream → provider rebuild), but the `StreamController` is not closed inside the test body in some variants — it is closed at `tearDown`. Per `test/README.md`, the controller should be closed inside the test body to avoid stream-adding-while-closed races.

---

## Summary

- **2 oversized test files** (1966 lines and 1133 lines) for a single 395-line source file — the biggest structural issue in the subdir.
- **1 critical fake-time violation** (4-second `pump` on line 1573) that directly inflates test run time.
- **~12 weak/smoke tests** in `modern_create_entry_items_test.dart` (property-access and no-assertion tests) that should be deleted or replaced with behavioral assertions.
- **6 copy-pasted DB setup blocks** in `modern_create_entry_items_test.dart`; collapsing them into one shared fixture would remove ~120 lines of boilerplate.
- **0 Glados candidates** — no pure stateless functions with structured inputs.
- **Top opportunity:** Merge `modern_create_entry_items_test.dart` into `create_entry_items_test.dart` (one file per source file), remove the 4-second pump, collapse DB setup blocks, and delete smoke tests. This alone would reduce the combined test file size by ~800 lines and cut ~4–5 seconds from the test run time.
